headline: Elon Musk Called His Father 'Evil': 'He Was Such a Terrible Human Being'
summary: Elon Musk has frequently expressed his disapproval of his father Errol Musk.

headline: Musk Could Pay The $1,000 'Trump Account' Bonus For Every Baby Born In The Next 4 Years And Keep 98% Of His Fortune
summary: Several billionaires have stepped up to help fund Trump accounts for kids in America. Elon Musk is not ready to help out, even though he could.

headline: Charlie Munger Credited Berkshire Hathaway's Success To This 'Huge Advantage' And 'Sensible People' At The Helm
summary: Charlie Munger: Berkshire Hathaway's biggest edge is lack of bureaucracy at headquarters, letting it move faster than rivals. Munger sees bureaucracy as a cancer and a waste in successful institutions. Amazon's CEO shares the view and vows to slash layers.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Carnival, Caterpillar, Meta — And Markets Make Modest Gains
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: PYPL, DJT, MU And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week (Dec. 15 to Dec. 19) on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets: PYPL, DJT, MU, RIVN, and NKE.

headline: Gene Munster Argues OpenAI Is 'Undervalued' Even At $830 Billion As Losses Mount And Big Tech Doubles Down: 'Mind-Numbing Reality Of AI's Potential'
summary: Gene Munster argues that OpenAI remains undervalued even at a rumored $830 billion valuation, saying its explosive growth, massive user scale and Big Tech's all-in commitment to AI outweigh mounting losses, hype concerns and dot-com-style risks.

headline: Ford Recalls Over 270,000 Electric And Hybrid Vehicles Over Potential Roll-Away Risk
summary: Ford Motor Co. (NYSE: F) has announced a recall of over 270,000 electric and hybrid vehicles in the U.S. due to a potential roll-away risk.

headline: Bernie Sanders Warns Billionaires Are Buying Elections As Pro-AI Super PACs Target Candidates Advocating AI Safety, Transparency: 'Cannot Be Alllowed'
summary: Sen. Bernie Sanders warned that wealthy tech investors and pro-AI super PACs, including groups backed by Meta and Trump allies, are poised to spend hundreds of millions in the 2026 elections to defeat candidates pushing for AI safety and transparency, arguing that billionaires must not be allowed to buy elections.

headline: Trump Secures Drug Price Cuts From 9 Major Pharma Firms Including Bristol Myers, Gilead, Merck In Medicaid Deal
summary: Trump and nine major pharmaceutical companies struck deals to lower drug prices for cash payers and Medicaid, with over $150 billion pledged for U.S. R&D and manufacturing.

headline: China's Power Capacity Surpasses US— Elon Musk Calls It 'Major Competitive Disadvantage'
summary: China's power generation capacity now exceeds the U.S., with rapid expansion and renewable energy growth giving it a strategic edge in AI, according to reports and Elon Musk.

headline: Elon Musk Wins Appeal As Delaware Supreme Court Restores 2018 Tesla Pay Package Now Worth $139 Billion: 'Vindicated'
summary: The Delaware Supreme Court reinstated Elon Musk's 2018 Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) compensation plan on Friday, reversing a lower-court ruling.

headline: SoftBank Scrambles To Raise $22.5 Billion For OpenAI By Year-End, May Tap Arm Stake To Back Debt As AI Spending Pressure Mounts: Report
summary: SoftBank is reportedly racing to assemble $22.5 billion in funding for OpenAI by year-end, leaning on asset sales, potential borrowing, and a sweeping pullback in other investments.

headline: Starbucks Taps Amazon Tech Veteran Anand Varadarajan As New CTO To Power Turnaround
summary: Starbucks Corp. (NASDAQ: SBUX) on Friday named Amazon veteran Anand Varadarajan its new chief technology officer, starting Jan. 19.
